facade_colour: warm red-orange brick with buff/cream brick voussoirs and accents

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, shared roofline with central gable, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and fenestration pattern with segmental openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

city_data.residential_floors: All floors (1–3) – residential building on commercial land

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red-orange brick with buff/cream polychromatic brick voussoirs and string course accents
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Brick largely intact; second-floor balcony added with metal railings; some weathering
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor: 2 windows in bay projection with segmental arches. Second floor: 3 windows with segmental arched heads and buff brick voussoirs. Third floor (gable): 1 small window. Metal balcony railings added at second floor.
photo_observations.cornice_details: No visible cornice; roofline transitions directly to gable
photo_observations.bay_window_details: First-floor projecting bay window visible on facade, consistent with HCD description of mirrored facades with first storey bay windows. Three-sided canted bay with segmental-arched window openings.
photo_observations.porch_details: Open porch with low concrete stoop and metal railings at ground-floor entrance.
photo_observations.chimney_details: Single brick chimney visible at roof ridge, right side.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Polychromatic brickwork with buff/cream brick voussoirs above windows; decorative brick in gable
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Metal balcony railings added at second floor level. Otherwise retains original brick cladding, voussoirs, and fenestration pattern.
photo_observations.notes: Semi-detached Victorian pair; photo shows left half (122). Lawn in front with walkway.

notes: Semi-detached Victorian pair; photo shows left half (122). Lawn in front with walkway.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 3, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, shared roofline with central gable, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and fenestration pattern with segmental openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Semi-detached three-storey house-form in warm red-orange brick with buff/cream brick voussoirs and accents with a gable roof. Features include a ground-floor canted, decorative bargeboards and polychromatic brickwork. Metal balcony railings added at second floor level. Otherwise retains original brick cladding, voussoirs, and fenestration pattern. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, shared roofline with central gable, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and fenestration pattern with segmental openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
